evidence: HMS Kempenfelt was a C-class destroyer built for the Royal Navy in the early 1930s.
Kempenfelt was transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy (RCN) in 1939 and renamed HMCS Assiniboine.
During World War II, she served as a convoy escort in the battle of the Atlantic.
sinking one German submarine by ramming, on anti-submarine patrols during the invasion of Normandy.
Kempenfelt displaced 1,390 long tons at standard load and 1,901 long tons at deep load.
The ship mounted four 45-calibre 4.7-inch Mark IX guns in single mounts.
Speed Grapher is a 2005 anime series created by Gonzo.
The series ran for 24 episodes from April to October 2005 on TV Asahi.
It tells the story of former war photographer, Tatsumi Saiga and his quest to save Kagura Tennōzu.
Saiga soon becomes entangled in this secret underground society.
Auto Club Speedway, formerly California Speedway, is a two-mile low-banked D-shaped oval superspeedway in Fontana, California.
The speedway has a grandstand capacity of 68,000 and 28 skyboxes.
Construction of the track began in 1995 and was completed in late 1996.
In April 1995, the San Bernardino County Board of Supervisors unanimously approved the project.
Scottish art is the body of visual art made in what is now Scotland, or about Scottish subjects, since prehistoric times.
The earliest examples of art from what is now Scotland are highly decorated carved stone balls from the Neolithic period.
The influence of the Renaissance can be seen in stone carving and painting from the fifteenth century.
Towards the end of the century Romanticism began to influence artistic production.
Tracheal intubation is the placement of a flexible plastic tube into the trachea to maintain an open airway.
It is frequently performed in critically injured, ill, or anesthetized patients.
The most widely used route is orotracheal, in which an endotracheal tube is passed through the mouth.
Intubation is normally facilitated by using a conventional laryngoscope.

evidence: Antara is an Indonesian news agency organized as a private company under the Ministry of State-owned Enterprises.
Antara began to produce its reporting independently of the government and was reorganized as a state-owned enterprise in 2007.
Antara was established on 13 December 1937 in Batavia (later Jakarta), the colonial capital of the Dutch East Indies.
The agency remained under private management until it was placed under the control of the presidency in the 1960s.

The Hogettes were a group of 12 male Washington Redskins fans who wore women's dresses, garden party hats, and pig snouts for two purposes: to cheer on their favorite NFL team and to raise money for charity.
They have raised over US $100 million for these and other charitable causes.
The group was founded in 1983 by Michael Torbert and became a regular fixture at Redskins games for nearly 30 years.
After the conclusion of the 2012 NFL season, the Hogettes announced they would be retiring their iconic outfits after 30 years of appearances.
The Admiral Hipper class was a group of five heavy cruisers built by Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine in the mid-1930s.
Only Prinz Eugen continued to serve in full commission and stayed in the Baltic until the end of the war.
Admiral Hipper and Blücher took part in Operation Weserübung, the invasion of Norway in April 1940.
She was ceded to the US Navy, which ultimately expended the ship in the Operation Crossroads nuclear tests in 1946.
The North American AJ Savage was a carrier-based medium bomber built for the United States Navy by North American Aviation.
The AJ-1 first became operational in 1950 and several were based in South Korea during 1953 as a deterrent against the Communists.
The aircraft was designed shortly after World War II to carry atomic bombs and this meant that the bomber was the heaviest aircraft thus far designed to operate from an aircraft carrier.
A contract for three XAJ-1 prototypes and a static test airframe was awarded on 24 June 1946.
A period 1 element is one of the chemical elements in the first row of the periodic table of the chemical elements.
The first period contains fewer elements than any other row in the table, with only two: hydrogen and helium.
The periodic table is laid out in rows to illustrate recurring trends in the chemical behaviour of the elements as their atomic number increases.
In terms of vertical trends down groups, helium can be seen as a typical noble gas at the head of Group 18.

DAGs can also represent collections of events and their influence on each other.

In graph theory, a pseudoforest is an undirected graph in which every connected component has at most one cycle.
A pseudotree is a connected pseudoforest.
Pseudoforests also form graph-theoretic models of functions and occur in several algorithmic problems.
Their matroid structure allows several other families of sparse graphs to be decomposed as unions of forests and pseudoforests.

The well-preserved keep is of a unique design and probably based on Byzantine architecture.
The design of the keep was unique, and has been termed 'one of the most remarkable keeps in England' by historian R. Allen Brown.
Much of the interior is built with high-quality ashlar stonework.
Either / Or is the first published work of the Danish philosopher Søren Kierkegaard.
It outlines a theory of human development in which consciousness progresses from an essentially hedonistic, aesthetic mode to one characterized by ethical imperatives.
Either / Or portrays two life views, one consciously hedonistic, the other based on ethical duty and responsibility.
The ethical life view is written as two long letters, discussing moral responsibility, critical reflection, and marriage.

The B & O offered such luxuries in the 1920s as onboard secretaries, barbers, manicures, and valets.

She performed convoy escort duties during the First World War.
12472: The I-351-class submarine was a class of tanker/transport submarines.
The IJN designed these submarines to support flying boats.

evidence: Blood Money is a side-scrolling shooter video game developed by DMA Design.
The game began development immediately after Menace.
Players move through four stages on the planet, taking control of a different vehicle in each level.
Each vehicle features unique weaponry, which players use to attack all advancing enemies.
The 1st Parachute Battalion was a parachute infantry battalion of the Australian Army.
Despite achieving a high level of readiness, the battalion did not see action during the war.
Efforts to raise an operational parachute capability in the Australian Army began in November 1942.
The battalion's personnel were mainly drawn from volunteers from other Army units.
Jane Williams was best known for her association with the Romantic poet Percy Bysshe Shelley.
Though she never had a romantic relationship with Shelley, he became deeply infatuated with her.
Jane was raised in England and India, before marrying a naval officer.
After Edward and she left England for Italy, they became acquainted with Percy and Mary Shelley.
H-13 is a county-designated highway in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan.
The roadway runs through rural national forest lands providing access to several campgrounds and parks.
H-13 starts at an intersection with US Highway 2 west of Moss Lake.
The road meanders from there northeasterly through rural Delta County forests.
'The Abducted' is the seventh episode of the third season of the American science fiction drama television series Fringe.
The episode followed the Fringe team's investigation of 'the Candyman'.
Reviews of the episode were mixed; multiple critics lauded the final five minutes of the episode.
The episode first aired on November 18, 2010, in the United States to an estimated 4.85 million viewers.

evidence: The International Association of Business Communicators (IABC) is a global network of communications professionals.
IABC hosts World Conference, a three-day event with professional development seminars and activities.
IABC's predecessor was the American Association of Industrial Editors (AAIE), which was founded in 1938.
In 1982 the association formed the IABC Research Foundation.
Membership grew 7 – 9 percent each year in the 2000s due to an increasing number of practitioners in the field of internal communications.
Dominique Louis Antoine Klein served in the French military during the French Revolutionary Wars and the Napoleonic Wars as a general of cavalry.
Klein was appointed general of division in 1799 and crossed the Rhine at Kehl.
Klein's cavalry played critical roles in the battles of Austerlitz and Jena and Auerstadt.
By 1795, Klein was a brigadier general in the Army of Sambre-et-Meuse.
'Who Do You Love?' is a song written by American rock and roll pioneer Bo Diddley.
The song represents one of Bo Diddley's strongest lyrical efforts.
The song has been interpreted and recorded by numerous musicians in different styles.
Guitarists' contributions to the various renditions of the song have been noted by music critics and writers.
The 1999 – 2000 South-West Indian Ocean tropical cyclone season was the first on record in which two storms struck Mozambique.
The most notable storm of the season was Eline, which was the longest-lasting storm on record in the basin.
Collectively, the three storms killed at least 316 people.
Eline caused around 700 deaths and about $500 million in damage.

evidence: Diệm made a state visit to the United States in 1957.
The visit was mainly celebratory and ceremonial.
Diệm was praised as a leader of a 'free country' in the midst of the Cold War.
The AFV successfully lobbied the media to write favourable reports on the South Vietnamese leader.
Bruix was one of four Amiral Charner-class armored cruisers built for the French Navy in the 1890s.
She served in the Atlantic Ocean, the Mediterranean, and in the Far East before World War I.
At the beginning of the war in August 1914, Bruix was assigned to protect troop convoys from French North Africa to France.
She was briefly assigned to support Allied operations in the Dardanelles in early 1915.
Cauldron is a two-dimensional (2D) shoot 'em up/platformer computer game developed by Palace Software.
Players control a witch, who aims to become the 'Witch Queen' by defeating an enemy called the 'Pumpking'.
Cauldron began development as a game based on the 1978 film Halloween.
Brown took the game in a new direction inspired by the Halloween holiday.
Jim Moran is a former U.S. Representative for Virginia's 8th congressional district.
Moran served from 1991 to 2015, and is a member of the Democratic Party.
Moran was born in Buffalo, New York, and grew up in Natick, Massachusetts.
He attended Marian High School in Framingham, Massachusetts.
A BLT is a type of bacon sandwich made up of four ingredients: bacon, lettuce, tomato and bread.
The essential ingredients are bacon, lettuce, tomato and bread.
The sandwich has a high sodium and fat content.
Due to this, low-fat mayonnaise is a common substitute along with low salt bread.

evidence: Doug Ring was an Australian cricketer who played for Victoria and Australia in 13 Tests from 1948 to 1953.
In 129 first-class cricket matches, he took 426 wickets bowling leg spin.
Ring made his Test debut against India in the 1947 – 48 season and was picked for Australia 's tour of England in 1948.
He had greater success against West Indies in 1951 – 52, and South Africa the following season.
The Raid on Unadilla and Onaquaga was a series of military operations by Continental Army forces and New York militia against the Iroquois towns.
In early October 1778, more than 250 men under the command of Lieutenant Colonel William Butler descended on the two towns.
The raid was executed in retaliation for a series of raids on frontier communities led by Mohawk chief Joseph Brant.
With the failure of British General John Burgoyne 's campaign to the Hudson after the Battles of Saratoga, the American Revolutionary War in upstate New York became a frontier war.
The 824th Tank Destroyer Battalion was a tank destroyer battalion of the United States Army active during the Second World War.
It saw service during 1944 – 45 in the European Theater of Operations.
After fighting through France and southern Germany, the battalion ended the war in Austria.
The battalion was originally formed in August 1942 in Oklahoma, and trained in the United States for two years.
The Grammy Award for Best Hawaiian Music Album was an honor presented to recording artists from 2005 to 2011 for quality Hawaiian music albums.
Prior to its creation, Hawaiian music recordings were eligible for the Best World Music Album category but no Hawaiian musician or group had ever won a Grammy Award.
Daniel Ho holds the record for the most wins, with five.
In 2011, the Recording Academy announced the retirement of the award category.
'You're Gonna Love Tomorrow' is the fifth season premiere episode of the American comedy-drama series Desperate Housewives.
The episode was written by series creator Marc Cherry and directed by Larry Shaw.
According to Nielsen ratings, the episode drew over 18 million viewers, making it the most-watched show of the night.
The time jump refreshed the show.

evidence: You Are the Apple of My Eye is a 2011 Taiwanese Romance film.
The film stars Ko Chen-tung as Ko Ching-teng, a prankster and a mischievous student.
The film was filmed almost entirely on location in Changhua County.
The lyrics of 'Those Years', the film's main theme, were written by Giddens.
Their relationship grows, and Ching-teng's grades gradually improve.
Ching-teng calls Chia-yi long-distance almost every night from university.
Smallville is an American television series developed by writer-producers Alfred Gough and Miles Millar.
The series follows Clark Kent in the fictional town of Smallville, Kansas.
Smallville, which ended its tenth and final season on May 13, 2011, follows Clark Kent before he becomes known as Superman.
The series received accolades ranging from Emmys to Teen Choice Awards.
Smallville was primarily filmed in and around Vancouver, British Columbia.
After Smallville's fifth season, The WB and UPN merged to form The CW.
The Auxois is a horse breed from eastern France.
Members of the breed are solid and muscular in appearance.
The Auxois is a direct descendant of the Bourguignon of the Middle Ages.
The average height is 15.3 to 16.3 hands.
World War I interrupted efforts to set the breed standard.
The breed reached its peak in the 1930s but began to decline with the advent of mechanization.
The 65th Boat Race took place on 4 April 1908.
Cambridge won by two-and-a-half lengths in a time of 19 minutes 20 seconds.
The Boat Race is a side-by-side rowing competition between the University of Oxford and the University of Cambridge.
The rivalry is a major point of honour between the two universities.
Cambridge were reigning champions, having won the previous year's race.
The race was first held in 1829 and has taken place on the Championship Course on the River Thames.
Urnula craterium is a species of cup fungus in the family Sarcosomataceae.
It is parasitic on oak and various other hardwood species.
Urnula craterium was first described in 1822 by American botanist Lewis David de Schweinitz.
The genus name means 'little urn'.
The fruit bodies develop on dead wood after it has fallen to the ground.
It produces bioactive compounds that can inhibit the growth of other fungi.

the cruisers were to play a key role in the Night Battle Force portion of the 'Decisive battle' strategy.
The Decisive Battle concept was the IJN's primary strategy for defeating the navies
The strategy consisted of four main components: a decisive surface fleet engagement determined by big guns.

McGee was the winner of Spike TV's eleventh season of The Ultimate Fighter in 2010.
In March 2010, McGee was announced as part of the eleventh season of The Ultimate Fighter
McGee narrowly defeated Seth Baczynski after going to a sudden victory round.

It is known only from the Pacific Northwest region of North America.
The fruit bodies of the fungus have a slimy dark chestnut-brown cap
The mushroom produces a rusty-brown spore print.

It is now the only extant species in the genus Nephrops.

Due to its ecological demands for particular sediments, N. norvegicus has a very patchy distribution.

evidence: Halt im Gedächtnis Jesum Christ is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
He composed it in Leipzig for Quasimodogeniti, the first Sunday after Easter.
The center of the cantata is the Easter hymn 'Erschienen ist der herrlich Tag'.
Bach structured the work in seven movements, arranged in symmetry around the central chorale.
'Forever and a Day' is a song by American recording artist Kelly Rowland.
It was produced and recorded by Jeberg at his personal recording studios in Copenhagen, Denmark.
Critics mainly praised the song for Rowland's vocals, as well as an infectious and refreshing production.
An accompanying music video features the iPad interface and scenes of Rowland partying with friends in Los Angeles.
Waptia fieldensis is an extinct species of arthropod from the Middle Cambrian Burgess Shale Lagerstätte of Canada.
It grew to a length of about 8 cm and resembled modern shrimp.
It is currently classified as a stem group crustacean and tentatively included in the clade Crustaceomorpha.
Psilocybe yungensis is a species of psychedelic mushroom in the Strophariaceae family.
It is used by Mazatec Indians in the Mexican State of Oaxaca for entheogenic purposes.
The species was described as new to science by American mycologists Rolf Singer and Alexander H. Smith.
Major General Sir Howard Karl Kippenberger was an officer of the New Zealand Military Forces who served in the First and Second World Wars.
He was appointed to command the 20th Battalion.
Howard Kippenberger was born on 28 January 1897 in Ladbrooks, in the Canterbury Region of New Zealand.

evidence: Nightshade is an action video game developed and published by Ultimate Play The Game.
The player assumes the role of a knight who sets out to destroy four demons in a plague-infested village.
The game features flip-screen and isometric gameplay.
The knight may be hit up to three times by an enemy, however the fourth hit will result in a life being deducted.
The Presidency of Ulysses S. Grant began during the turbulent Reconstruction period following the American Civil War.
Grant bolstered the Executive Branch's enforcement powers by signing into law the Department of Justice.
Grant expanded federal authority that protected African American civil rights against domestic terrorism in the South.
Grant worked hard to ensure the passage of the Fifteenth Amendment that gave black men the right to vote.
Delaware Route 273 (DE 273) is a state highway in New Castle County, Delaware.
The route runs from Maryland Route 273 (MD 273) at the Maryland border near Newark east to DE 9 and DE 141 in New Castle.
What is now DE 273 was originally built as a state highway in the 1920s and 1930s.
DE 273 was designated by 1936 to connect the Maryland border near Newark to Hares Corner.
Brien McKeiver Taylor is a former pitcher in minor league baseball.
He spent seven seasons in the minor leagues, primarily with the New York Yankees organization.
Taylor was chosen by the New York Yankees with the first overall selection in the 1991 Major League Baseball Draft.
He is one of two players to be picked first overall in the MLB draft to never reach the major leagues.
State Route 527 (SR 527) is a state highway in Snohomish County, of the U.S. state of Washington.
It extends 9.23 miles north from I-405 in the city of Bothell, Washington.
The highway itself did not fully open until October 17, 1969.
A large construction project to widen the highway and put up sound walls ran from 1991-2006.

evidence: Rossia was an armored cruiser of the Imperial Russian Navy built in the 1890s.
She served as a long-range commerce raider during the Russo-Japanese War.
Rossia was originally intended to be a repeat of the armored cruiser Rurik.
She was considered to be a good sea boat with a smooth roll.
Macmillan was an activist for women's right to vote, and for other women's causes.
She was one of the founders of the Women's International League for Peace and Freedom.
She was the first female science graduate from the University of Edinburgh.
Macmillan studied science subjects including Honours Mathematics.
Tropical Storm Beryl was the strongest off-season Atlantic tropical cyclone on record to make landfall in the United States.
It quickly weakened to a tropical depression, dropping heavy rainfall while moving slowly across the southeastern United States.
Beryl developed on May 26 from a low-pressure system offshore North Carolina.
The storm became extratropical on May 30.
HMS Hurricane was an H-class destroyer that had originally been ordered by the Brazilian Navy.
The ship was torpedoed by a German submarine on Christmas Eve 1943.
Hurricane displaced 1,350 long tons at standard load and 1,883 long tons at deep load.
She was powered by Parsons geared steam turbines, driving two shafts.
Eisenhower National Historic Site preserves the home and farm of Dwight D. Eisenhower.
The farm served as a weekend retreat for the President and a meeting place for world leaders.
The property was purchased by then-General Eisenhower and his wife Mamie in 1950.
It became the Eisenhowers' home after they left the White House in 1961.

evidence: German submarine U-255 was a Type VIIC U-boat that served in Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine during World War II.
The submarine was laid down on 21 December 1940 at the Bremer Vulkan yard at Bremen-Vegesack.
One of the most successful U-boats to operate in Arctic waters.
She operated from Norway during 1942 – 1943, and then from France in 1944 – 1945.
Tropical Storm Debby caused extensive flooding in North Florida and Central Florida during late June 2012.
The storm dropped immense amounts of precipitation near its path.
Rainfall peaked at 28.78 inches (731 mm) in Curtis Mill, Florida.
Overall, Debby caused at least $250 million in losses and 10 deaths.
'Bizounce' is the debut single recorded by American singer Olivia, from her debut studio album Olivia (2001).
It was released on March 20, 2001 as the lead single from the album.
Olivia revealed her disappointment that 'Bizounce' was chosen and released as the lead single.
Olivia was the first artist signed to J Records by Clive Davis.
SM UB-16 was a German Type UB I submarine or U-boat in the German Imperial Navy during World War I.
UB-16 spent her entire career in the Flanders Flotilla and sank 23 merchant ships.
UB-16 was ordered in November 1914 and was laid down at the AG Weser shipyard in Bremen in February 1915.
UB-16 was converted into a minelayer with the replacement of her torpedo tubes.
HMS Ramillies was a Royal Sovereign-class pre-dreadnought battleship of the Royal Navy.
She was built by J. & G. Thompson at Clydebank, starting with her keel laying in August 1890.
She was armed with a main battery of four 13.5-inch guns and a secondary battery of ten 6-inch guns.
The ship had a top speed of 16.5 knots.
